meta.description: A contained world. A sky with edges. A story drawn in, not imposed.
meta.rootDescription: The 22 Proto-Sinaitic glyphs. The hub language spoken before the Collapse.
meta.collapseDescription: The one language shattered into pieces. The Pankus recorded it as a legal covenant. The storm god Tarḫunt departed and has not returned.
meta.sourceText: The Nešatta Codex · Pankuš Pittiyaš Ḫalki · The Assembly Covenant of the Grain/Foundation

realms.name: Sūrya Samrājya
realms.territory: Maine coast, New Brunswick, Nova Scotia — the eastern shore where the sun rises from the ocean before anywhere else in Telumë

founder.name: Enki
founder.title: First priest-king
founder.description: Listener. Heard the Tower hum before it fell.
founder.tablet: Tablet of Mantra — sound, chant, alignment

government.description: The Sun-King is a living god, reincarnated each generation. He does not rule — he radiates.

internalSchism.description: The Sun-Court worships the king as divine. The Fire-Purists worship the flame itself and reject the monarchy.

realms.glyphReading: Glyphs are mantras — seed syllables that align the chakras and open doors of perception.
realms.collapseTelling: The Division of Peoples (Manuṣyāṇāṃ Vibhedaḥ). Humanity tried to return to heaven. The gods scattered them. The Sunfolk preserved the original tablet.
realms.sacredGeography: Bay of Fundy tides — the ocean breathes here. The Sun-King appears at dawn on the water's edge.

realms.name: Ealdor
realms.territory: North of the St. Lawrence, Toronto-Montreal corridor, boreal Shield, Manicouagan enclave, western islands (Georgian Bay fringe), Labrador coast
realms.people: Three peoples — The Markfolk (highland core), The Fellfolk (northern coast), The Cernfolk (western islands)

founders.name: Ymir
founders.title: Primordial screamer
founders.note: No tablet — the Saga. Oral tradition only.

government.description: The Witenagemot (meeting of wise men) chooses the king. The king rules until death or incompetence.

manicouaganEnclave.name: The Ring
manicouaganEnclave.description: Meteor crater as sacred wound. Three traditions, one site, a religious order that serves all three.
manicouaganEnclave.ealdorReading: Where Hengist's first oath was sworn

realms.glyphReading: Glyphs are oaths — carved on standing stones, binding reality.
realms.collapseTelling: The Broken Covenant. One clan withheld their stone. The oath shattered. Language shattered with it.

realms.name: Kichigamswak Confederacy
realms.nameBreakdown: Kitchi/Kichi (great, sacred) + Gam (lake/sea) + -swak (Algonquian animate plural suffix)
realms.nameMeaning: We who *are* the great lake, plural, animate, alive. Not 'people who live near the great lake.' The political body and the geographic body are the same thing.
realms.territory: Great Lakes basin, Ottawa River valley, Montreal corridor
realms.capitalNote: No fixed capital. The council fire moves between the seven clan-seats.

founder.name: Cadmus
founder.title: Outsider-translator
founder.description: Arrived by river from the east. Found the Great Law already existing. Copied it the way he copied the letters — not as inventor, but as the first outsider to recognize its value.
founder.tablet: Tablet of Letter — not Cadmus's invention. His *translation* of the Kichigamswak Great Law into a script the outside world could read.

government.description: Consensus council. No single ruler. Decisions require clan agreement.

externalScript.name: Cadmus layer
externalScript.description: Phoenician-derived script used for external diplomacy only, never internal governance

realms.canonExplosive: The Helorian Republic's entire legal tradition is a translation of the Kichigamswak Great Law filtered through Cadmus's hand. The Senate knows. They have never admitted it.
realms.glyphReading: Glyphs are living beings — animate, like the water. The Adzmyst sacred seven are not marks. They are presences.
realms.collapseTelling: The Great Law predates the Tower. The confederacy was governing itself before the Tower was built. The Collapse was not their event — it happened to other people, far away, and sent refugees to their shores.

realms.name: The Haldorian Republic
realms.territory: Gulf of St. Lawrence / Bay of Ealdforn — Gaspé Peninsula, Anticosti Island, Magdalen Islands, Cape Breton, Miramichi mouth, north shore Quebec, scattered archipelagos
realms.capitalNote: None. The Pankus assembly meets at Nešatta every seven years.
realms.people: No single people — seven named city-states, each its own culture

founder.description: Refused one. The Pankus Accord was brokered by the last Hittite elder — not a founder, a facilitator.

government.description: Decisions advisory. Trade-binding clause is the only enforcement mechanism.
government.foundingDocument: The Nešatta Codex · Pankuš Pittiyaš Ḫalki
government.foundingDocumentDescription: Seven clay tablets. Three translatable (Hittite + Luwian). One missing (removed by Tsarnigrad). One unreadable. One sealed. One blank — the covenant is not finished.

oldestLayer.name: Anatolian
oldestLayer.description: Pre-Tower archive at Nešatta. The glyph claim.

aegeanLayer.name: Macedonian Greek
aegeanLayer.description: Northern Greek, aggressive, looked down on by southern city-states

anatolianGreekLayer.name: Phrygian
anatolianGreekLayer.description: Pre-classical Anatolian-Greek interface. Old money, old gods.

maritimeLayer.name: Epic Latin
maritimeLayer.description: Pre-imperial maritime Latin. The language of the net and the oar, not the sword and the senate.

realms.canonExplosive: The Hittite archive at Nešatta contains tablets predating the Tower with recognizable Adzmyst glyphs. If authentic — the glyphs didn't scatter at the Collapse. They were stolen from the Anatolian tradition to build the Tower. The Collapse was punishment for theft, not hubris.
realms.glyphReading: Glyphs are legal facts. The Nešatta archive treats them as evidence.
realms.collapseTelling: Recorded in the Nešatta Codex as a legal covenant between the gods and the assembly. The storm god Tarḫunt departed. The seventh tablet is blank. The Pankus waits.

cityStates.name: Nešatta
cityStates.character: Oldest. Archive city. Pankus fire-keeper. Nearly abandoned. Holds the glyph archive that could break every other kingdom's origin story.
cityStates.population: Skeleton crew of priests and archivists

cityStates.name: Radogost
cityStates.character: Trade heart. Glad host. Inland gateway to the river routes. Where the Pankus gets its grain.

cityStates.name: Tihomir
cityStates.character: Witness city. Monastery. Doesn't vote. Keeps the written record. Bogumil's home.

cityStates.name: Tsarnigrad
cityStates.character: Dark fortress. Almost-monarchy. Greatest internal threat to the Pankus covenant. The city that effaced and recarved the 'no king' inscription.

cityStates.name: Aristopol
cityStates.character: The city of the best. Empire-builder. Perpetually proposes a hegemon. Exhausting and indispensable. Named themselves after a quality they assigned to themselves.

cityStates.name: Gordéon
cityStates.character: Old money. Old gods. The unsolvable city. Arrived with wealth nobody can account for. Doesn't explain itself. The Gordian knot as political theology.

cityStates.name: Pescassa
cityStates.character: The fishing city. Maritime underclass Latin — the language of the net and the oar. Arrived in boats. Never left. Named with Latin root + Anatolian suffix from centuries of trading with Nešatta.

nešattaCodex.title: Pankuš Pittiyaš Ḫalki
nešattaCodex.translation: The Assembly Covenant of the Grain/Foundation
nešattaCodex.scribe: Bogumil, Tihomir monastery, Year 847 of the Haldorian Reckoning
nešattaCodex.originals: Clay tablets, now lost or sealed

1.name: Ḫantezziš (The First Thing)
1.content: The creation, the Tower, the prohibition of kings

2.name: Luwian Intrusion
2.content: The sun god's triple position, the chiasm, the storm god's departure

3.name: Ḫalkiš (The Grain/The Foundation)
3.content: The triple covenant, the assembly entrusted to the land, the closing seal

4.note: Removed by a delegation from Tsarnigrad who came in the night and left before morning

5.note: Script unknown to Bogumil — not Hittite, not Luwian, not any hand he was trained to read

6.note: Intact but sealed with clay impressed with a glyph Bogumil refused to reproduce

7.note: Clay fired. Surface prepared. Nothing written. Bogumil: 'I do not know what this means. The abbot says it means the covenant is not finished. The storm god has not returned to write the final tablet.'

nešattaCodex.centralLine: LUGAL-uš lē ēšta — a king shall not be
nešattaCodex.centralWord: Ḫalki — grain and foundation are the same word
nešattaCodex.closingSeal: 𒁹𒀭𒊏 𒁹𒋾𒀭 𒁹𒀭𒍪 · PITTIYA · PITTISSA · PITTAZU — The covenant was. The covenant broke. The covenant holds.

sharedIncantation.ealdor: Þæt fyr hæld. Þæt duru āþnaþ.
sharedIncantation.gloss: The fire holds. The door opens.
